message:
feat(harness): bootstrap differential automaton from VDBE bytecode

DifferentialAutomaton gains a from_vdbe_ops() constructor that
translates a narrow supported subset of compiled VDBE programs into
the existing differential operator pipeline. The supported shape is:

  Rewind -> [optional equality filter] -> Column projection -> ResultRow -> Next

The translation extracts:
- Projection columns from consecutive Column opcodes targeting the
  ResultRow register window
- Optional equality filter from the Column/literal-load/IsNull/Ne
  four-opcode pattern that the codegen emits for WHERE col = <const>

An expected_input_width field is added to DifferentialAutomaton. When
set (always set by from_vdbe_ops), execute() validates that every
input row matches the expected column count, returning a new
SchemaChanged error variant if the schema has drifted since the
automaton was compiled. This catches column additions/removals that
would silently corrupt differential results.

decode_vdbe_literal_load() handles Integer, Int64, Real, String8, and
Null opcodes, extracting the literal value from P1/P4 as appropriate.

New error variants in DifferentialPlanError:
- SchemaChanged { expected_width, actual_width }
- UnsupportedProgram { detail }

Tests cover:
- Projection-only bootstrap (SELECT name FROM users)
- Filter+projection bootstrap (SELECT name FROM users WHERE id = 2)
- Rejection of ORDER BY programs (sorter not supported)
- Schema width drift detection
- NULL equality filter rejection

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
